title.value: Catalytic Asymmetric Axially Chiral Allenyl C−P Bond Formation

abstract.value: Chiral organophosphorous compounds are very important in catalysis, organic syntheses, and medicinal chemistry. However, catalytic enantioselective protocols for the axially chiral allenyl phosphorus compounds have never been reported. Herein, a palladium-catalyzed enantioselective carbon−phosphorus bond formation reaction affording axially chiral allenyl phosphonates has been developed. The reaction enjoys high yields and ees accommodating a wide range of functional groups. Mechanistic studies have unveiled an overwhelming kinetic resolution process.

warnings: The key substrates are propargylic benzoates/carboxylates, but no exact matching substrate label is available in the allowed list, so `substrate` is set to `not specified`.
The leaving group is primarily benzoate/carboxylate, but no exact benzoate/carboxylate leaving-group label is available in the allowed list, so `leaving_group` is set to `not specified`.
The chiral ligand is a Trost-type diphosphine ligand, but no exact matching ligand class is available in the allowed list, so `ligand_or_chiral_source` is set to `not specified`.
The phosphorus coupling partner is an H-phosphonate/phosphite rather than a listed organometallic partner, so `organometallic_partner` is set to `not specified`.
